title: LSTM Gates Playground
tagline: Drag the forget/input/output gates, watch the cell state flow
summary: Three sigmoid gates protect an LSTM's cell state — the forget gate decides what to erase, the input gate what to write, the output gate what to read. Drag each slider, feed in a sequence, and watch the cell state change timestep by timestep. The 'conveyor belt' intuition becomes literal.
highlights: Drag f_t (forget), i_t (input), o_t (output) from 0 to 1.
Feed a 10-step input sequence; see the cell state evolve like a conveyor belt.
Saturate f = 1 and i = 0 — the cell state is frozen, memory preserved indefinitely.
Saturate f = 0 — every timestep erases; the network forgets everything.
Switch to the 'vanilla RNN' mode to see how gradients collapse without gating.
